tluaMetatableIndexTable.tlua(21,20): error TLUA2339: Property 'nothing' does not exist on type '{ describe: (self: table) => string; count: number; n: number; }'.
tluaMetatableIndexTable.tlua(26,7): error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
tluaMetatableIndexTable.tlua(31,1): error TLUA2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaMetatableIndexTable.tlua(48,7): error TLUA2322: Type 'string | number' is not assignable to type 'number'.
  Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.


==== tluaMetatableIndexTable.tlua (4 errors) ====
    // setmetatable pairs a table with its metatable: a read the table misses falls through to
    // the metatable's __index, so the call cannot keep returning the plain table it was given.
    
    local Helpers = {
      describe = function(self: table): string return "d"; end,
      count = 0,
    };
    
    local mt = { __index = Helpers };
    
    local obj = setmetatable({ n = 1 }, mt);
    
    // The table's own members stay.
    local n: number = obj.n;
    
    // A missing member is found behind __index.
    local d: string = obj.describe(obj);
    local c: number = obj.count;
    
    // What neither has is still missing.
    local absent = obj.nothing;
                       ~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'nothing' does not exist on type '{ describe: (self: table) => string; count: number; n: number; }'.
    
    // The table wins a collision: a raw read hits it before the metatable ever runs.
    local collide = setmetatable({ v = 1 }, { __index = { v = "str" } });
    local cn: number = collide.v;
    local cs: string = collide.v;
          ~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
    
    // A fallback member is writable at its own type -- a Lua write raw-sets the table, which
    // shadows the metatable's copy from then on.
    obj.count = 2;
    obj.count = "two";
    ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    
    // An index signature behind __index carries over.
    declare idxMt: { __index: { [key: string]: number } };
    local ix = setmetatable({ n = 1 }, idxMt);
    local iv: number = ix["anything"];
    
    // The table only wins while it *answers*: a key that is absent or nil is exactly what makes
    // Lua run the metatable, so a member that admits nil reads as its own non-nil half or the
    // fallback's value. Defaults behind __index -- the reason this matters -- work.
    declare partial: { name?: string; id: number };
    local defaulted = setmetatable(partial, { __index = { name = "anonymous" } });
    local always: string = defaulted.name;
    
    declare nilable: { v: number | nil };
    local either = setmetatable(nilable, { __index = { v = "str" } });
    local both: number | string = either.v;
    local notJustNumber: number = either.v;
          ~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'string | number' is not assignable to type 'number'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    
    // An intersection still says what its __index is.
    declare interMt: { __index: { a: number } } & { __tostring: () => string };
    local inter = setmetatable({ n = 1 }, interMt);
    local ia: number = inter.a;
    
    // The result is still the table it was built from.
    interface HasN { n: number }
    local asTable: HasN = obj;
    
    // ...and a named interface that also spells the fallback out.
    interface Described { n: number; describe: (self: table) => string; count: number }
    local asDescribed: Described = obj;
